summary: End-to-end testing of GRAV-SHIFT (FastAPI + React canvas game). Backend: 20/20 pytest cases pass covering health, /scores POST/GET, name normalization, validation, /scores/rank, sorting/limit, and _id leakage. Frontend: Menu, leaderboard fetch (rendered 10 rows with seeded NEO/TRINITY/MORPHEUS 1), play->HUD, pause/resume, mute toggle, natural game-over flow, and score submission all work end-to-end via the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

issues: Submit-Score button is enabled even when final score is 0; backend accepts score=0 so a 0-point entry can be persisted. Consider disabling submit when score === 0 to avoid leaderboard noise.

Row layout uses tight letter-spacing causing rank/name/score to visually collide on narrow widths (e.g. '#01TEST157389999990' read as concatenated in textContent). Visual rendering is fine due to grid columns, but consider explicit gaps for screen readers.

action_items: (Optional UX) Disable submit-score-button when score === 0 to prevent zero-score submissions.
(Optional code quality) Game.jsx is 952 lines — consider splitting into engine (loop/draw/physics) and UI (overlays) modules for maintainability.
(Optional) Add SR-only/aria-label gaps for leaderboard rank/name/score so accessibility tooling reads them as separate fields.
critical_code_review_comments: Game.jsx exceeds 700-line guideline (952 lines). Split into Game engine logic and UI overlay components.

Web Audio: setMuted/isMuted use module-level globals. This works but means React state and audio module state can drift (e.g. on hot-reload). Consider making audio state a single source of truth or syncing on mount.

ScoreCreate accepts score=0 — combined with no auth, this means anyone can spam empty-name-passing 0-score entries. Currently the validator filters empty names, so spam risk is limited; consider rate-limiting or minimum-score for production.

Leaderboard component fetches once on mount; opening it from gameover after a successful submit will not show the just-submitted score until reopened (fetch effect deps are []). Working as designed in current flow but worth noting.
Game.jsx imports unused `containerRef` (assigned but never read) — minor cleanup.
seed_data_creation: Test entries created via POST /api/scores: TEST_A=10, TEST{epoch}=9999990, NEOTRIM=1, HCKER!=2, TESTSORTA=50, TESTSORTB=500, TESTSORTC=5000, TESTPW=0. These use the TEST/NEOTRIM/HCKER prefixes and can be cleaned up later if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_gravshift_api.py cover all leaderboard/validation paths and run in ~1.7s. Public URL is in frontend/.env. Game-over can be reached naturally in ~15-20s by NOT flapping (gravity drops bird to floor). Mute toggle behaves correctly when clicked once; rapid Playwright force-clicks may fire multiple pointer events that toggle twice — prefer JS .click() in tests. Pre-seeded entries (NEO=42, TRINITY=105, MORPHEUS 1=67) are present along with new TEST entries.
rca of the issue: No real bugs found. One initial Playwright failure on mute-toggle was due to multiple pointer events from rapid force-click; verified with JS click that the SOUND ON/OFF toggle works correctly. No backend or frontend issues require fixes.
